description: Baseline measurement of full_pipeline_batch_25 benchmark (98,024 ns)
Removed message.clone() in Hydrator::hydrate_message; extracted owned fields before consuming message. Result: 90,234 ns (7.9% improvement).
Removed double clone of enriched_records in process_batch_internal by passing references. No significant change (90,273 ns vs 90,234 ns baseline after first optimization). Within noise.
Changed hydrate_messages to sequential processing, avoiding FuturesUnordered overhead. Result: 86,305 ns (additional ~2% improvement over 88k).
Pre-sized HashSet with exact capacity (messages.len() for DIDs, messages.len()*2 for URIs). Result: 87,295 ns, slightly slower than 86,305 ns. Within noise but not beneficial.

Converted TurboCache methods (get/set + checks) from async to sync to eliminate async overhead. Result: 86,563 ns, not significantly better than sequential baseline 86,305 ns. Slight regression, likely within noise.
Removed #[instrument] attributes from hydrate_message and hydrate_batch to eliminate tracing overhead in release builds. Result: 85,759 ns (additional ~0.6% improvement).
Replace HashSet with Vec + sort/dedup for unique DIDs/URIs collection. Result: 86,494 ns, worse than baseline 85,759 ns. Discard.
Pass references to store_batch/publish_batch instead of cloning enriched_records. Result: 86,703 ns vs 85,759 ns baseline. Slight regression, discard.
Run store and publish sequentially instead of using tokio::join!. Result: 87,297 ns vs 85,759 ns baseline. Regression.
Change set_user_profile/set_post to take &str instead of String to avoid double allocation. Result: 86,394 ns, worse than best 85,759 ns. Discard.
Removed TTL from MokaCache to avoid expiration checks. Result: 86,943 ns vs best 85,759 ns. Regression, discard.
Added #[inline] to hydrate_message and hydrate_messages. Result: 86,734 ns vs best 85,759 ns. Regression.
Latest measurement: 87,687 ns, within noise but above best. No further improvements identified.
